danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy construct that appears mid-level during vertical-scrolling segments
danger_signals.meaning: Marks transition into high-danger 'Danger' area; signals environmental threat requiring escape

danger_signals.visual_form: Guardian creatures at palace entrances and dragon bosses at vertical level conclusions
danger_signals.meaning: Major world-state threat; defeat required to progress; highest immediate danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles from airborne and ground enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player survival; contact causes life loss and power-down

danger_signals.visual_form: Shift from top-view aerial to side-scrolling palace perspective
danger_signals.meaning: World-state change from open-sky environment to enclosed indoor danger

safety_signals.visual_form: 'P' icons hidden in containers and weapon capsules
safety_signals.meaning: Safety enhancement; increases player combat capability and survivability
Resource for gaining continues; signals safe resource zones within levels

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden treasure chest zones accessible during vertical-scrolling segments
safety_signals.meaning: Safe reward spaces; optional detours offering score and resource accumulation

safety_signals.visual_form: Fourth power-up state that transforms player character appearance
safety_signals.meaning: Maximum safety state in NES version; grants damage reduction (withstands 2 hits) and enhanced firepower

faction_indicators.faction: Player Forces (Wings of Love and Courage)
faction_indicators.color_identity: Pink/Green (Michelle Heart variant), Blue (Kevin Walker), White wings (Japanese version), Golden wings (overseas version)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magical wings granted by Ares; represents divine authorization to oppose Dark
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: NES version features character as Turtle Dove form at power-up tier 4
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in sky during vertical segments and in palace interiors during horizontal segments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Giant mechanical face represents Dark's presence; palace architecture signifies Dark's domain
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types (airborne and ground-based) serve as Dark's manifestations; guardian bosses protect dark's sanctum
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Palaces at end of each vertical segment; dragon bosses in NES version; central final boss represents core threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Treasure chests in Lucky areas; weapon containers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Optional level detours; resource caches scattered throughout world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Top-down vertical scrolling perspective; open environment with airborne enemies and ground targets below
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player flies with magical wings; can shoot guns at airborne targets and drop bombs on ground enemies; free vertical movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift to side-scrolling horizontal perspective; enclosed architectural spaces with vertical platforms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player walks, crouches, climbs ladders, jumps; uses ground-based combat; confined movement within palace corridors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guardian boss encounter at palace entrance; visual marker of sanctum boundary
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory combat encounter; defeat required for progression; triggers transition from aerial to ground combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant mechanical face appears mid-level; visual signal of environmental hazard
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must escape dangerous area or face consequences; optional high-risk/high-reward zone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual weapon changes: single gun → twin laser → penetration beam → three-way flame → Psycho Flame (arcade) or Turtle Dove transformation (NES)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased firepower, speed, and attack range correlate with visual weapon upgrades; player becomes visually more capable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character reverts to weaker visual form after being hit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Power-downs cascade; player loses accumulated upgrades; returns to baseline combat capability

environmental_storytelling.example: Each stage culminates in a palace, visually representing Dark's strongholds; palace complexity signals advancing threat level
Airborne enemies in sky segments vs. ground-based enemies in palace segments communicate environmental danger profiles
Guardian creatures at palace entrances signal checkpoint importance; dragon bosses in NES version at vertical-level conclusions emphasize each stage's climax
Lucky areas hidden within levels but visually accessible; Danger zones clearly marked by giant mechanical face, communicating risk/reward trade-offs
Progression from single-shot to multi-directional fire to Psycho Flame/Turtle Dove mode tells story of escalating player empowerment against Dark's forces
Transition from aerial (surveying battlefield) to ground-level (infiltration) communicates shift from reconnaissance to direct action within enemy territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Single-shot gun; normal speed; basic bomb capability; can be shot down easily
Enhanced firepower (multi-directional shots), increased speed, improved bomb mechanics; visually distinct appearance
Transforms into Turtle Dove; shoots wide-area attacks 4x stronger than default; withstands 2 hits before downgrade; signals peak combat capability
Engage player in sky segments; fire projectiles; must be shot down; destruction may yield weapon power-ups
Encountered in palace segments; vulnerable to bombs from aerial segments; represent ground-level threats during infiltration
Mandatory defeat required for palace access; signals major world-state transition; triggers shift to ground-based combat
Appears at end of each vertical segment in NES version; represents escalating threat; triggers final boss transition
Encountered after palace infiltration completion; represents civilization-level danger; requires complete victory for world-state restoration

notes: Legendary Wings communicates world-state primarily through perspective shifts and environmental architecture. The game uses visual form-changes (weapon upgrades, Turtle Dove transformation) to signal player empowerment progression. Danger is communicated through enemy density, boss placement hierarchy, and the giant mechanical face transition trigger. The duality of aerial vs. ground combat creates a spatial narrative where the player transitions from reconnaissance (sky) to infiltration (palace). NES version enhancements (additional dragon bosses, extended levels, flying in horizontal segments) further develop world-state communication through expanded environmental storytelling. Faction identity relies on wing symbolism (player agency) vs. mechanical/architectural symbols (Dark's control). Optional zones (Lucky/Danger areas) communicate risk-reward world-state variations within each stage.
